I've added user @eukaryo as instructed in #9.

Hi! This is the friendly automated conda-forge-webservice.

I've added user @y0z as instructed in #11.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

Please contact [conda-forge/core](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/maintainer_faq.html#mfaq-contact-core) to have this PR merged, if the maintainer is unresponsive.

Checklist before merging this PR: - [ ] Dependencies have been updated if changed: see [upstream](https://github.com/optuna/optuna) - [ ] Tests have passed - [ ] Updated license if changed and `license_file` is packaged Information about this PR: 1. Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. 2. The bot will almost always only open one PR per version. 3. The bot will stop issuing PRs if more than 3 version bump PRs generated by the bot are open. If you don't want to package a particular version please close the PR. 4. If you want these PRs to be merged automatically, make an issue with @conda-forge-admin,`please add bot automerge` in the title and merge the resulting PR. This command will add our bot automerge feature to your feedstock. 5. If this PR was opened in error or needs to be updated please add the `bot-rerun` label to this PR. The bot will close this PR and schedule another one. If you do not have permissions to add this label, you can use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerun bot in a PR comment to have the `conda-forge-admin` add it for you. Dependency Analysis -------------------- Please note that this analysis is **highly experimental**. The aim here is to make maintenance easier by inspecting the package's dependencies. The bot will close this PR and schedule another one. If you do not have permissions to add this label, you can use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerun bot in a PR comment to have the `conda-forge-admin` add it for you. Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

Please contact [conda-forge/core](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/maintainer_faq.html#mfaq-contact-core) to have this PR merged, if the maintainer is unresponsive.

Hi! This is the friendly automated conda-forge-webservice.

I've added user @contramundum53 as instructed in #35.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

Please contact [conda-forge/core](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/maintainer_faq.html#mfaq-contact-core) to have this PR merged, if the maintainer is unresponsive.

I've added user @himkt as instructed in #37.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

I've added user @knshnb as instructed in #39.

If you want these PRs to be merged automatically, make an issue with @conda-forge-admin,`please add bot automerge` in the title and merge the resulting PR. This command will add our bot automerge feature to your feedstock. 5. If this PR was opened in error or needs to be updated please add the `bot-rerun` label to this PR. The bot will close this PR and schedule another one. If you do not have permissions to add this label, you can use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerun bot in a PR comment to have the `conda-forge-admin` add it for you. Dependency Analysis -------------------- Please note that this analysis is **highly experimental**. The aim here is to make maintenance easier by inspecting the package's dependencies. Importantly this analysis does not support optional dependencies, please double check those before making changes. If you do not want hinting of this kind ever please add `bot: inspection: false` to your `conda-forge.yml`. If you encounter issues with this feature please ping the bot team `conda-forge/bot`. Analysis by source code inspection shows a discrepancy between it and the the package's stated requirements in the meta.yaml. ### Packages found by source code inspection but not in the meta.yaml: - wandb - tensorflow-estimator - tornado - redis-py - gpytorch - pytorch-ignite - arm_pyart - bokeh - pytorch - plotly - botorch - jsonnet - tensorflow - lightgbm - scikit-learn - xgboost - tensorboard - mlflow - scikit-optimize - keras - autovizwidget - allennlp - skorch - chainer - pytorch-lightning - cma This PR was created by the [regro-cf-autotick-bot](https://github.com/regro/cf-scripts). The **regro-cf-autotick-bot** is a service to automatically track the dependency graph, migrate packages, and propose package version updates for conda-forge.
